78: **Côte d'Ivoire**, level with one other team on 6 points in **Group I** with their position not yet separated, open the day at 10:00 AM PT against **Norway**, who sit 2nd in **Group I** on the same 6 points. The two sides arrive at this knockout tie as the closest-matched pairing of the round — separated only by a single goal in their respective group-stage goal differences — making this a genuine 50-50 with enormous knockout stakes on the line.
77: **France**, the runaway winners of **Group I** on a flawless 9 points, meet **Sweden** at 2:00 PM PT — a **Sweden** side that finished 3rd in **Group F** on 4 points and will need everything to go right against the tournament's most convincing group-stage performers. **France**'s firepower and defensive organisation have looked near-impossible to breach; **Sweden** must disrupt their rhythm early and make this a battle of attrition if they are to pull off one of the round's biggest upsets.
79: **Mexico**, perfect through the group stage as **Group A** leaders on 9 points, close the evening at 6:00 PM PT against **Ecuador**, who advanced as 3rd in **Group E** on 4 points. The gulf in points flatters neither side — **Mexico** have been relentless going forward with a goal difference of +6, while **Ecuador**'s resilience and set-piece threat make them genuinely dangerous against any opponent. Playing in front of a home-nation crowd that will lift the roof, **Mexico** are heavy favourites, but **Ecuador** have every reason to believe.
76: **Japan** have never won a knockout match in tournament history — four attempts, four exits (Turkey 2002, Paraguay on penalties 2010, Belgium 2018, Croatia on penalties 2022). Beating **Brazil** in Houston would be a genuinely historic first. The Seleção won **Group C** with 7 points after back-to-back 3-0 wins over **Haiti** and **Scotland**, while **Japan** went unbeaten in **Group F** (W1 D2, 5 points) — only the second time in their history they've survived the group stage without a loss. Vinícius Jr has scored in all three group matches, just the fifth Brazilian to achieve that at this tournament. **Japan** will be without Takefusa Kubo (knee) and face a late fitness test on centre-back Kō Itakura (injured before half-time against **Sweden**); **Brazil** are missing Raphinha (hamstring). Carlo Ancelotti's side have won 11 of 14 all-time meetings, but **Japan** stunned them 3-2 in an October 2025 friendly in Tokyo — rallying from 2-0 down at the break for their first-ever win over the five-time champions. Manager Hajime Moriyasu's 3-4-2-1 shape, built on defensive compactness and lightning-fast transition play, is designed for exactly this kind of knockout occasion.
74: This is **Germany**'s first knockout match in 12 years — they were eliminated in the group stage at both the 2018 and 2022 tournaments, and the last time they reached a knockout game was the 2014 final, which they won. Julian Nagelsmann's side topped **Group E** with 10 goals from seven different scorers, the most balanced attack in the competition. Deniz Undav has three of those without starting a match — the tournament's most prolific substitute. But **Germany** lost their final group game 2-1 to **Ecuador**, and have conceded in nine consecutive tournament matches, tying a team record. Centre-back Nico Schlotterbeck is out for the tournament with a knee injury suffered against **Côte d'Ivoire**; Antonio Rüdiger steps in. **Paraguay** advanced as third-place qualifiers after a bruising 4-1 loss to **USA** in the opener, but steadied themselves with a 1-0 win over **Türkiye** and a goalless draw with **Australia** — back-to-back clean sheets built on coach Gustavo Alfaro's compact 4-5-1. They'll be without Miguel Almirón (red card vs **Türkiye**). Nagelsmann called them 'an uncomfortable opponent' who 'defend well and stay compact.' **Paraguay**'s last knockout appearance was 2010, when they beat **Japan** on penalties before falling to **Spain** in the quarterfinals.
75: **Netherlands**, the outright winners of **Group F** on 7 points, close the evening at 6:00 PM PT against **Morocco**, who are level with one other team on 7 points from their own group, their exact position not yet separated. In a remarkable twist, this match falls exactly 32 years to the day since **Netherlands** beat **Morocco** 2-1 at USA '94 — also on American soil — and current Dutch coach Ronald Koeman started that 1994 fixture. **Morocco**'s 2022 semi-final run remains the finest finish by an African nation in tournament history; **Netherlands** are three-time finalists still chasing a first title. Both sides carry identical points hauls but contrasting identities — Dutch attacking dominance against Moroccan defensive solidity and lethal transitions. This one has the quality and the stakes to define the entire round.
